title: Prompt Engineering MCQ Quiz
subtitle: Multiple-choice questions based on the 2025/26 Ynov Paris course slides on prompt engineering, AI limits, weak prompts, and the five elements of an effective prompt.

questions.question: Which statement best matches the course definition of artificial intelligence?
options: A search engine that only returns existing web pages
A database that only stores information without producing new results
A human-like mind that understands the world exactly as a person does
A system that analyzes data and performs tasks usually requiring human intelligence
questions.explanation: The correct answer reflects the course definition: AI analyzes data and performs tasks such as writing, answering, image creation, or idea generation. The human-like mind option is misleading because the course states that AI does not think or understand the world like a human. The database and search-engine options are too narrow.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: In the course workflow, what happens immediately after a prompt is written?
options: The AI stores the answer as a fixed truth
The AI analyzes the request
The AI automatically knows the hidden intention
The AI asks a human teacher for validation
questions.explanation: The sequence is: a prompt is written, the AI analyzes the request, predicts the most probable answer, then generates a result. Hidden intention is not automatically known, and the course does not describe teacher validation or fixed truth storage.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: What is the main goal of the session described in the course?
options: To replace all human communication work with automated tools
To compare paid AI subscriptions
To teach only image-generation commands
To identify frequent prompt errors and structure requests more effectively
questions.explanation: The course goal is to identify common errors, understand context, structure requests, and apply good practices for more precise and relevant answers. The other choices reduce the course to unrelated or overly narrow aims.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Why does the course emphasize prompt engineering?
options: Because AI refuses to answer short prompts in all cases
Because AI output quality depends strongly on the information supplied
Because prompts are only useful for coding tasks
Because AI can infer all preferences without being told
questions.explanation: The course explains that clearer, more precise, contextualized input improves the relevance of AI responses. Short prompts are not always refused, prompt engineering is not only for coding, and AI cannot infer all preferences.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Which item is part of the course agenda?
options: Legal regulation of copyright
The five elements of a good prompt
Installation of local machine-learning models
Financial analysis of AI companies
questions.explanation: The agenda includes objectives, exercises, errors with AI, and the five elements of a good prompt. The other topics are not presented as agenda items in the PDF.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Which description best defines a prompt in the context of this course?
options: A visual filter applied to generated images only
A request or instruction given to AI to produce a result
A database table used to organize outputs
A password used to access an AI account
questions.explanation: A prompt is the instruction or request that guides AI output. The other choices confuse prompts with authentication, databases, or image-only settings.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: What does the course imply about AI reasoning?
options: AI predicts likely responses based on data rather than understanding like a human
AI always reasons with the same awareness as a human expert
AI cannot produce text because it only processes numbers
AI understands every social situation without context
questions.explanation: The course states that AI predicts the most probable response and does not understand the world like a human. The other options attribute human understanding or incorrect limitations to AI.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Which outcome is most likely when a prompt lacks clarity?
options: A response that becomes automatically more creative
A response that refuses all marketing topics
A response that is generic or poorly oriented
A response that always follows a table format
questions.explanation: The course repeatedly links vague or weak prompts to generic, poorly adapted, or less usable answers. The other options are not supported by the course.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of prompt engineering in this lesson?
options: The process of programming an AI model from scratch
The act of choosing an AI brand before writing anything
The practice of structuring AI requests using role, context, mission, constraints, and format
The use of only long prompts regardless of purpose
questions.explanation: The lesson focuses on improving prompts through five elements. Programming a model, choosing a brand, or making every prompt long is not the same as prompt engineering.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Which course aim is connected to professional usefulness?
options: Avoiding all constraints in prompts
Memorizing the names of every AI tool available
Obtaining responses that are more precise, relevant, and adapted to expressed needs
Using the same prompt for every task
questions.explanation: The stated aim is better precision, relevance, and adaptation. Memorizing tools, avoiding constraints, or reusing one prompt in every case would not meet that aim.
questions.category: Foundations of AI and prompt engineering

questions.question: Which list contains only generative AI uses mentioned in the course?
options: Hardware repair, battery charging, screen cleaning, printing
Calendar reminders, GPS navigation, spreadsheet sorting, email storage
Text generation, image creation, video creation, code generation
Accounting audits, passport control, weather measurement, banking transfer
questions.explanation: The course lists text, image, video, code, music, and data-analysis uses. The other lists include tasks not presented as the main generative AI uses in the lesson.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which tool is given as an example of a generative AI tool in the course?
options: Photoshop only
Excel only
Notion only
Midjourney
questions.explanation: The course names tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Midjourney. Photoshop, Excel, and Notion may use AI features in some contexts, but they are not the examples listed in the course slide.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Why can AI respond badly according to the course?
options: It is designed to ignore every detailed instruction
It cannot guess intentions, context, expectations, or preferences that were not provided
It always prefers short answers over relevant answers
It only answers correctly when prompts are written in code
questions.explanation: The course explains that AI lacks access to unstated intentions, context, level of requirement, and personal preferences. The other options exaggerate or misrepresent the reason for poor answers.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which missing element is most directly responsible for a generic answer to a communication request?
options: The color of the AI interface
The date of the computer purchase
Context about the project, audience, and objective
The number of tabs open in the browser
questions.explanation: For a communication request, project context, target audience, and objective help shape the answer. Interface color, browser tabs, and unrelated purchase dates do not guide the response meaningfully.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: What does the phrase 'the AI predicts the most probable answer' imply?
options: The AI secretly reads the user's mind
The AI never changes its answer across contexts
The output is generated from patterns and supplied information, not guaranteed human understanding
The output is always objectively correct
questions.explanation: Prediction means the system produces likely output from data and prompt information. It does not guarantee correctness, mind reading, or fixed answers across different contexts.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which factor is NOT listed as something AI automatically knows?
options: The user's context
Personal preferences
The exact price of every product in every store
The user's intentions
questions.explanation: The course specifically lists intentions, context, level of requirement, and personal preferences as unknown unless supplied. Product prices are also not automatically known, but they are not part of the course list, making this the best answer to the wording.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which statement best connects AI limitations to prompt writing?
options: Because AI lacks hidden context, prompt writing must make relevant information explicit
Because AI is a database, prompts should contain only keywords
Because AI understands everything, prompts should avoid detail
Because AI only produces images, prompts should not mention text
questions.explanation: The correct statement follows directly from the course: missing context leads to weaker answers, so information must be made explicit. The other statements contradict the course definition and examples.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which expected result matches a weak prompt such as 'Make an Instagram campaign'?
options: A complete campaign with verified business data
Highly personalized to budget, tone, target, and duration
A guaranteed viral campaign plan
Too generic, poorly adapted, not necessarily usable
questions.explanation: The course explicitly describes the probable response to a weak Instagram prompt as too generic, poorly adapted, not very creative, and not necessarily usable. The other options assume information not supplied.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which use of AI is mentioned alongside text, images, videos, code, and music?
options: Physical delivery
Medical diagnosis as a guaranteed service
Data analysis
Bank account creation
questions.explanation: The course includes data analysis among the principal uses. The other options are not listed as core uses in the slide and some imply risky guarantees.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: Which principle best explains why two prompts on the same topic can produce very different outputs?
options: Longer prompts are automatically correct
The role, context, mission, constraints, and format can orient the response differently
AI always chooses outputs randomly
The topic alone fully determines the answer
questions.explanation: The five prompt elements can strongly change the answer even when the subject is the same. Randomness, topic-only determinism, or length alone do not explain the course examples.
questions.category: Generative AI uses and AI limitations

questions.question: What is the main problem with the prompt 'Talk to me about digital marketing'?
options: It contains too many constraints
It gives too much audience context
It asks for a table with too many columns
It is broad and does not specify the action, scope, or output format
questions.explanation: The prompt is vague because it does not define a clear mission, scope, or format. The other options describe features that are absent from the prompt.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which version is closest to the improved prompt shown for digital marketing?
options: Give everything about social media in any format
Analyze the main digital marketing trends in 2026 and present them as a list with concrete examples
Make digital marketing better
Talk about marketing digital quickly
questions.explanation: The improved version specifies the action, scope, year, structure, and examples. The other versions remain broad, unclear, or lacking a format.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which element is added by 'under 100 words' in a prompt about SEO?
options: A brand identity
A role
A constraint
A target platform
questions.explanation: 'Under 100 words' limits the length of the answer, so it is a constraint. It does not define a role, brand identity, or platform.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: In the improved Instagram campaign prompt, what does 'expert in social media' provide?
options: A final format
A budget limit
A campaign duration
A role for the AI
questions.explanation: 'Expert in social media' gives the AI an identity and professional perspective. Budget, format, and duration are represented by other parts of the prompt.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which part of the improved Instagram prompt functions as context?
options: Dynamic tone
A natural cosmetics brand for 18-25 year olds
Present the result in a table
Seven-day campaign
questions.explanation: The brand type and target audience describe the situation, so they are context. Table is format, dynamic tone is a constraint, and seven-day campaign is part of the mission or scope.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which combination makes a prompt more exploitable according to the course?
options: Only personal preferences without a task
Only a broad topic and no limits
Specific action, useful context, constraints, and expected format
Only a tool name and no mission
questions.explanation: A usable prompt combines a clear mission with context, constraints, and format. Broad topics, tool names alone, or preferences without a task do not guide production enough.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which prompt is best aligned with the course's good-practice model?
options: Make something UX about students.
Improve this app.
Say if the app is good or bad, no details needed.
As a UX designer, analyze this student app for onboarding problems and return a table with issue, impact, and recommendation in beginner-friendly language.
questions.explanation: The correct prompt defines role, mission, context, format, and constraint. The other choices are vague, lack context, or restrict useful explanation.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which diagnosis best fits 'Make me a logo'?
options: It already contains all five prompt elements
It lacks context about brand, audience, style, constraints, and expected deliverable
It is only weak because it mentions design
It is too technical for AI to understand
questions.explanation: The prompt gives a mission but lacks essential details. It does not contain all five elements, is not inherently too technical, and design itself is not the problem.
questions.category: Weak versus improved prompts

questions.question: Which set contains the five elements of a good prompt taught in the course?
options: Budget, logo, font, image, video
Search, copy, paste, save, print
Role, context, mission, constraints, expected format
Title, subtitle, color, icon, password
questions.explanation: The course's five elements are role, context, mission, constraints, and expected format. The other sets mix unrelated interface or production elements.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What is the purpose of assigning a role to the AI?
options: To replace the mission with a job title only
To make the answer longer regardless of need
To guide vocabulary, posture, knowledge, and methods linked to an expertise domain
To prevent the AI from using any examples
questions.explanation: A role helps the AI adopt a relevant expert perspective. It does not automatically make answers longer, remove examples, or replace the need for a mission.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which prompt fragment best represents a role?
options: Return the result as an email
My audience is 18-25 years old
Use fewer than 150 words
Act as a communication professor
questions.explanation: 'Act as a communication professor' gives the AI an identity. The audience is context, word count is a constraint, and email is format.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Why can the same question 'How can a website be improved?' receive different answers from different roles?
options: AI ignores the role and always gives the same answer
The website topic prevents specialized answers
Each role focuses on different criteria such as conversion, usability, performance, or content
Roles only change grammar, not recommendations
questions.explanation: The course shows that a marketer, UX designer, developer, and web writer emphasize different aspects. The other options contradict that example.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which focus would most likely come from a UX designer role in the website example?
options: Customer acquisition only
Server-side performance only
Search engine keywords only
User experience and ergonomics
questions.explanation: The course states that a UX designer would focus on user experience and ergonomics. Acquisition aligns more with marketing, performance with development, and keywords with web writing or SEO.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which focus would most likely come from a developer role in the website example?
options: Brand ambassadors on campus
Technical performance and accessibility
First-order discounts
Natural cosmetics campaign tone
questions.explanation: The course links the developer role to technical performance and accessibility. The other options belong to communication campaign examples, not the developer perspective.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What is the purpose of context in a prompt?
options: To remove the need for a mission
To hide the real goal from AI
To help AI understand the situation, project, or objective
To force a single-word answer
questions.explanation: Context gives useful background about the situation, project, or objective. It does not hide goals, require single-word answers, or replace a clear mission.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which prompt fragment is context rather than a constraint?
options: Maximum 150 words
A responsible fashion brand for students with a limited marketing budget
No technical jargon
Professional tone
questions.explanation: The brand, audience, and budget describe the situation. Word count, tone, and jargon limits are constraints.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: In the brand communication example, which recommendation becomes possible because of added context?
options: Collaborating with student associations
Using every platform without priority
Only creating a generic blog
Ignoring the target audience
questions.explanation: With the student-focused eco-responsible brand context, collaboration with student associations becomes relevant. The other answers remain generic or unfocused.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What does the mission specify?
options: The action expected from the AI
The AI company's name
The visual color of the interface
The device used to type the prompt
questions.explanation: The mission defines what the AI must do, such as generate ideas, write an article, analyze a document, or create a presentation plan. The other options are unrelated.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which option is the clearest mission?
options: Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of this project
Professional tone
For beginner students
In a table
questions.explanation: 'Analyze the strengths and weaknesses' is a clear action. Tone, audience level, and table format guide the response but are not the mission itself.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Why are action verbs important in prompt writing?
options: They replace the need for any context
They make every answer shorter
They guide the type of production expected from AI
They automatically verify factual accuracy
questions.explanation: Action verbs such as write, analyze, and compare guide the output type. They do not guarantee factual verification, replace context, or always shorten answers.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which prompt fragment is a constraint?
options: Create a presentation plan
Act as a marketing expert
Without technical jargon
A brand for students
questions.explanation: 'Without technical jargon' limits style and complexity, making it a constraint. The other fragments define mission, context, or role.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What do constraints help prevent?
options: Responses that include any useful structure
Responses that are too long, too complex, or poorly adapted to the audience
Responses that contain concrete examples
Responses that match the required tone
questions.explanation: Constraints guide length, tone, level, and complexity. They are meant to improve adaptation, not remove structure, tone, or examples.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which prompt best uses constraints for a beginner audience?
options: Give SEO ideas in any style
Tell everything about SEO for experts only
Explain SEO in under 100 words, with a pedagogical tone and no technical jargon
Explain SEO completely with no limit and all technical details
questions.explanation: The correct prompt sets length, tone, and jargon constraints for beginners. The other choices either lack constraints or target an advanced audience.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What is the role of expected format in a prompt?
options: To tell AI what expertise to adopt
To specify the structure in which the answer should be delivered
To describe the user's project background
To define the ethical risks of AI
questions.explanation: Expected format defines the output shape, such as a table, detailed plan, LinkedIn post, or email. Role, context, and ethical risks are separate issues.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which prompt fragment defines the expected format?
options: Natural cosmetics brand for young adults
Budget limited
Return the answer as a table with columns: idea, estimated cost, potential impact
Act as a social media expert
questions.explanation: The table and column names specify the final structure. Budget is context or constraint, expert identity is role, and brand description is context.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Why is expected format useful according to the course?
options: It guarantees the answer contains no mistakes
It makes all prompts creative campaigns
It prevents any need for context
It makes the result easier to exploit and reuse
questions.explanation: The course says format helps obtain content directly adapted to its use and easier to reuse. It does not guarantee correctness or replace other prompt elements.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which classification is correct for the fragment 'dynamic tone'?
options: Role
Mission
Expected format
Constraint
questions.explanation: Tone is a stylistic constraint that frames how the response should be written. It is not the AI identity, task action, or structural output format.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which statement best compares constraints and expected format?
options: Constraints define only the AI's job title; format defines only the budget
Format controls accuracy; constraints control data storage
Constraints and format are identical terms
Constraints frame limits or style; expected format defines the final structure
questions.explanation: Constraints handle limits, tone, level, and style, while format defines the shape of output. The other statements confuse or falsely equate these elements.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: Which order is shown in the winning structure diagram?
options: Definition, tool, price, platform, signature
Mission, format, role, budget, conclusion
Context, answer, correction, image, table
Role, context, mission, constraints, expected format
questions.explanation: The course diagram shows the winning structure as role, context, mission, constraints, and expected format. The other orders include items outside the five-element structure.
questions.category: The five elements of a good prompt

questions.question: What is the objective of Exercise 1?
options: Memorize every AI tool on the market
Create a complete website without instructions
Transform a catastrophic prompt into a high-performing prompt
Delete all constraints from a prompt
questions.explanation: Exercise 1 asks for transforming weak starting prompts into effective prompts. The other goals are not part of the exercise.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which starting prompt appears in Exercise 1?
options: Write a legal contract for a company acquisition
Make me a presentation about ecology
Translate an entire novel
Summarize a private medical file
questions.explanation: The exercise lists starting prompts including a presentation about ecology, a logo, and an Instagram campaign. The other tasks are not listed.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which improved version best follows the five-step model for the ecology prompt?
options: Give some ecology information without structure.
Act as an environmental communication teacher. Prepare a 5-minute beginner presentation on daily ecological habits for high-school students, with 5 key points and simple examples.
Talk about ecology in any way.
Make ecology better.
questions.explanation: The correct version includes role, mission, audience context, constraints, and a clear structure. The other choices remain vague and difficult to exploit.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which improved version best follows the five-step model for the logo prompt?
options: Act as a brand designer. Propose 3 logo concepts for a student eco-fashion brand, minimalist style, two colors maximum, with a short rationale for each concept.
Find a brand image without explaining it.
Draw something pretty.
Make me a logo.
questions.explanation: The correct prompt gives role, context, mission, constraints, and format. The other prompts do not define brand, audience, style, number of ideas, or explanation.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which improved version best follows the five-step model for an Instagram campaign?
options: Talk about social networks.
Give random post ideas with no target.
Make a campaign Instagram.
Act as a social media expert. For a natural cosmetics brand targeting 18-25 year olds, create a 7-day Instagram campaign with a dynamic tone, limited budget, and a table format.
questions.explanation: This version mirrors the improved course example by adding role, context, mission, constraints, and format. The other options are vague and lack exploitable detail.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: What is the goal of Exercise 2?
options: Create a video game interface
Build a database schema
Create the best possible LinkedIn post for an alternance search
Write a restaurant menu
questions.explanation: Exercise 2 focuses on a LinkedIn publication for a student in digital creation seeking an alternance. The other tasks do not match the exercise objective.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which requirement belongs to the LinkedIn publication exercise?
options: Present the profile and highlight skills to attract recruiters
Produce a technical code file
Avoid mentioning skills or contact possibilities
Write only a private message to one friend
questions.explanation: The LinkedIn post must present the profile, highlight skills, attract recruiters, and encourage contact. The other choices contradict the exercise.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which prompt is best for the LinkedIn exercise?
options: Write anything about school.
Find recruiters for me.

Act as a career communication coach. Write a LinkedIn post for a digital creation student seeking an alternance next intake; highlight design and digital skills, use a professional but engaging tone, and end with a contact invitation.
Make LinkedIn good.
questions.explanation: The correct prompt contains role, context, mission, constraints, and an expected communication outcome. The other options lack the structure needed for a strong post.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which element is missing if a prompt says only 'Create the best LinkedIn post possible'?
options: A topic related to LinkedIn
A request for any output
Context about the student profile, skills, audience, and goal
A general mission to create a post
questions.explanation: The prompt includes a broad mission and topic but lacks profile context, skills, target recruiters, tone, and format. That missing context reduces relevance.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which choice best represents a 'which is NOT' understanding of the five steps?
options: Role gives the AI an identity
Format indicates the final form
Context gives useful information
Password is one of the five prompt elements
questions.explanation: Password is not one of the five elements. Role, context, and format are part of the course framework.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: A prompt asks: 'Act as a marketing expert. For a student clothing brand with a small budget, compare Instagram and campus events in a two-column table, maximum 120 words.' Which element is least explicit?
options: Role
Expected format
Context
Mission
questions.explanation: Role, context, constraints, and format are very explicit. The mission is present through 'compare,' but the exact decision purpose is less explicit than the other elements, making it the least explicit part.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: A prompt gives a role and a format but no project information. What is the most likely weakness?
options: The role will become useless in every case
The answer will be impossible to format
The AI will automatically know the project context
The answer may be structured but still generic
questions.explanation: A role and format can improve orientation and structure, but missing context can still produce generic recommendations. The other choices are too absolute or contradict the course.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which evaluation question best checks whether a prompt includes a mission?
options: Is the final answer in a table?
Is the audience background explained?
Is the action expected from AI clearly stated?
Is the tone limited to professional?
questions.explanation: The mission is the action expected from AI. Table format, tone, and audience background correspond to format, constraints, and context.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which evaluation question best checks whether a prompt includes constraints?
options: Is the project background explained?
Are limits such as length, tone, level, or jargon specified?
Is there a named expertise role?
Is there a topic at all?
questions.explanation: Constraints concern limits and style, including length, tone, level, and jargon. Role, background, and topic are different prompt components.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which answer best synthesizes the course's central lesson?
options: A strong prompt should be short and avoid all details
A strong prompt replaces the need to review the AI output
A strong prompt is only needed for image tools
A strong prompt turns implicit expectations into explicit instructions across role, context, mission, constraints, and format
questions.explanation: The central lesson is to make expectations explicit through the five-element structure. Shortness alone, image-only use, and skipping review are not supported by the course.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design

questions.question: Which prompt would likely produce the most adapted response for a beginner learner?
options: Act as a teacher. Explain prompt engineering to beginners in 120 words maximum, with a simple example and no technical jargon.
Write prompt engineering.
Explain prompt engineering fully.
Give every detail about AI models.
questions.explanation: The correct prompt provides role, audience, mission, constraints, and example requirement. The other prompts are too vague or too broad for a beginner-focused answer.
questions.category: Exercises and applied prompt design
